confirm_delete: Really delete? This action cannot be undone.

api_tokens.title: API Tokens
api_tokens.token_warning: This is the only time this token will be shown. Copy it now!
api_tokens.scope_required: At least one scope is required
api_tokens.revoke_confirm_message: This token will be permanently revoked and can no longer be used. This cannot be undone.

tools.no_types_selected: No backup types with configurable methods selected

nas.password_placeholder: Optional if SSH key is provided
nas.private_key_hint: Place the private key in /app/config/ssh/ and enter the path here. Leave blank to use password authentication.
nas.ssh_uses_main_credentials: SSH test uses the credentials entered above
nas.auto_shutdown: Automatically shut down NAS after backup
nas.supported_systems: Compatible: Synology, QNAP, TrueNAS, OpenMediaVault, Unraid and other Linux-based NAS systems
nas.ssh_key_deployed: SSH key successfully set up and deployed to NAS
nas.ssh_key_error: Failed to set up SSH key
nas.warning_unraid: ⚠️ Unraid: SSH keys do not survive a reboot. After a reboot the key must be set up again – or persisted manually in /boot/config/ (see prerequisites).
nas.warning_qnap: ⚠️ QNAP: SSH configuration may be overwritten by firmware updates. Check that the key is still active after updates.
nas.setup_hint_synology: Synology DSM: Enable SSH (Control Panel → Terminal & SNMP). Enable User Home Service (Control Panel → Users → Advanced → Enable User Home Service). User must be a member of the administrators group.
nas.setup_hint_qnap: QNAP QTS: Enable SSH (System Settings → Network & File Transfer → Telnet/SSH). No further steps required.
nas.setup_hint_truenas: TrueNAS SCALE/CORE: Start SSH service and enable Auto-Start (System → Services → SSH). The user home dataset must use Unix permissions (not pure NFSv4).
nas.setup_hint_omv: OpenMediaVault: Enable SSH (Services → SSH). User must be assigned to the _ssh group (Users → Groups).
nas.setup_hint_unraid: Unraid: Enable SSH (Settings → Security). ⚠️ SSH keys do not survive a reboot. For persistent setup: copy the key to /boot/config/ and load it via the go script on startup.
nas.setup_hint_linux: Linux (generic): Ensure PubkeyAuthentication yes in /etc/ssh/sshd_config, then run sudo systemctl restart sshd.

recovery.warning_message: This mode is ONLY intended for disaster recovery. Normal backup operations will be STOPPED.
recovery.no_database_containers: No database containers in this backup
recovery.verify_backup_description: Verify the integrity of this backup before restoring.
recovery.verification_failed_hint: Some files are corrupted or missing. Restore may be incomplete.
recovery.full_server_restore_confirm: This will restore ALL selected components. Continue?
recovery.restore_in_progress: Full server restore is running in the background...
recovery.reboot_required_flash: Reboot required to apply flash drive changes
recovery.scan_hint: Scan a backup directory to find manifests:
recovery.scan_no_results: No manifests found in the specified path
recovery.no_checksums_body: This backup was created without checksums (older format). Integrity cannot be verified automatically.
recovery.delete_backup_body: All files of this backup will be permanently deleted. Are you sure?
recovery.delete_backup_confirm_body: All files of this backup will be permanently deleted. This cannot be undone.
recovery.nas_verify_body: Backup files are on the remote NAS and cannot be accessed locally. Stored checksums cannot be verified.
recovery.nas_verify_ssh_failed_body: SSH connection to NAS failed. Checksums could not be verified.

setup.step_3: Recovery key MUST be stored securely
setup.warning_1: This key is the ONLY way to reset your password
setup.warning_2: Without this key: forgotten password = reinstall!
setup.warning_3: Store it in a safe place (e.g. KeePass, 1Password)
setup.confirm_downloaded: I have downloaded the recovery key
setup.confirm_understood: I understand that without this key, recovery is impossible

encryption.feature_2: Zero-knowledge: only you can decrypt your backups
encryption.warning_1: Forgotten password + lost recovery key = data PERMANENTLY lost!
encryption.warning_2: No backdoor, no reset without recovery key
encryption.password_hint: At least 12 characters, as complex as possible
encryption.recovery_warning_1: This key is the ONLY way to reset your password
encryption.recovery_warning_2: Without this key: encrypted backup + forgotten password = data lost
encryption.recovery_warning_3: Store it SAFELY (e.g. KeePass, vault, safe)
encryption.confirm_downloaded: I have downloaded the recovery key
encryption.confirm_understood: I understand that without this key, recovery is impossible

decryption.unlock_hint: This backup is encrypted. Enter your encryption password.

notifications.discord_hint: Server Settings → Integrations → Webhooks → New Webhook

guide.onboarding_text: Let's set up your first backup in a few steps. We'll create a backup target (where your data is stored) and a backup job (what gets backed up and when).
guide.step_target_desc: Where do you want to store your backups? Choose a local path, or a remote server or NAS over SSH.
guide.step_target_type_local_desc: Store backups on a local disk or mounted path (e.g. /unraid/user/backups). Fast and simple.
guide.step_target_type_nas_desc: Store backups on a NAS or Linux server via SSH and Rsync. Supports Wake-on-LAN and auto-shutdown.
guide.step_backup_desc: Select what you want to include in this backup job. You can always add more jobs later.
guide.backup_flash_hint: Your Unraid boot drive config. Small but critical — strongly recommended.
guide.backup_appdata_hint: Container data from /unraid/user/appdata. This is usually the most important data.
guide.backup_vms_hint: VM disk images and XML configs. Can be large.
guide.backup_docker_hint: Export Docker images as .tar archives. Useful for offline recovery.
guide.backup_sysconfig_hint: Network settings, users, plugins, and boot config export.
guide.step_schedule_desc: When should this backup run? You can change this later.
guide.step_done_text: Your backup target and job have been created successfully.
guide.abort_message: Your progress will not be saved. Really cancel?
guide.none_selected: No backup types selected — please select at least one.

gfs.title: GFS Retention
gfs.daily_hint: Keep last X days (one per day)
gfs.monthly_hint: Keep last X months (1st–3rd of month)
